name: Repro 07 — Loop "first item only" (FIXED — two patterns)

parameters.content:
### Pattern B — Run Once for Each Item

**Mode:** Run Once for Each Item
**Returns:** a single object *(not an array)*

```js
const lead = $input.item.json;
return { json: { ...lead, /* ... */ } };
```

Use when you want clean per-item logic and a strict 1-to-1 mapping. Slightly slower at high item counts.

### What you should observe

After clicking **Test workflow**, both branches should run in parallel:

| Node | Items out | ✓/✗ |
|---|---|---|
| Generate 5 Leads | **5** | ✓ |
| Process Lead (FIX A — items.map) | **5** | ✓ fix works |
| Process Lead (FIX B — runOnceForEachItem) | **5** | ✓ fix works |
| Mark as Synced (A) | **5** | ✓ all leads synced |
| Mark as Synced (B) | **5** | ✓ all leads synced |

If both branches show 5 items at every node, you've confirmed both fixes work and you understand the items model.

---

### One last check

Open `Process Lead (FIX B — runOnceForEachItem)` and look at the **Mode** dropdown at the top of the parameters panel. It says **Run Once for Each Item** — that's the only structural difference from Pattern A. The code body is shorter because the iteration is implicit.
